jokes.text: Oscar being asked to close the door for Michael's 'procedure' consultation
jokes.setup: Michael asks Oscar to close the door for what sounds like a serious business discussion
jokes.punchline: It's revealed he wants medical advice about a colonoscopy
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate behavior - treating a coworker like a medical consultant. Good setup/payoff structure with believable character motivation.

jokes.text: Michael asking Oscar about colonoscopy 'sensations or emotions'
jokes.setup: Michael needs advice about his colonoscopy
jokes.punchline: He asks about 'sensations or emotions' in overly clinical way
jokes.explanation: Michael's awkward formality about medical procedures is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Is there anything I can do to make it more pleasurable... for me or for Dr. Chaudry?
jokes.setup: Michael seeking advice about his medical procedure
jokes.punchline: He wants to make a colonoscopy 'pleasurable' for both parties
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriateness - completely misunderstanding the nature of medical procedures. The phrasing is perfectly awkward and quotable.

jokes.text: My main concern is should I have a safe word?
jokes.setup: Michael continuing to seek colonoscopy advice
jokes.punchline: He thinks he needs a safe word for a medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Michael's confusion about medical procedures. The economy is perfect - maximum discomfort in minimal words. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I'm gonna put it in the mail Sunday. You'll get it Wednesday.
jokes.setup: Jim offers to get something done by Sunday night instead of Monday
jokes.punchline: He'll mail it Sunday but it won't arrive until Wednesday - worse than the original deadline
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - Jim appears helpful but delivers something objectively worse. Classic Jim passive-aggressive humor.

jokes.text: Because we are the Three Amigos... one of the amigos will go off... to the bathroom while the other two have a secret meeting.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining he's not worried about being excluded from Jim and David's meeting
jokes.punchline: His analogy inadvertently reveals his insecurity about being excluded
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt to appear nonchalant backfires as his analogy reveals exactly what he's worried about. Good character-driven irony.

jokes.text: Pam: 'I think the pregnancy really brought us together'
jokes.setup: Michael asking about her relationship with Jim
jokes.punchline: Pam's dry response about pregnancy bringing them together
jokes.explanation: Pam's understated response to an obvious situation. The deadpan delivery makes it funny.

jokes.text: If you're lying to me right now, your baby is gonna come out a liar. That's how it works. They inherit things through the breast milk.
jokes.setup: Michael interrogating Pam about what Jim told her
jokes.punchline: He threatens her with completely made-up 'science' about inheritance through breast milk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael pseudo-science delivered with complete confidence. The absurdity of threatening an unborn baby with inherited lying is peak Michael logic.

jokes.text: Michael: 'They inherit things through the breast milk'
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his lying baby theory
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of how traits are inherited
jokes.explanation: Michael's confident delivery of completely wrong medical information. The breast milk detail makes it more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Pam's horrified reaction to Michael discussing her breast milk
jokes.setup: Michael's inappropriate comment about breast milk inheritance
jokes.punchline: Pam's visible discomfort and request to stop talking about it
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat to Michael's inappropriateness. Necessary for the rhythm but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Dwight questioning how the ladder ended up on top of Darryl if he fell off it
jokes.setup: Darryl explains his injury story
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately spots the logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Dwight's suspicious nature and logical thinking catching an obvious lie. Good detective comedy setup.

jokes.text: I didn't know we were in communist Sweden. If we were, I wouldn't have to worry, 'cause we'd have universal health care.
jokes.setup: Darryl tells Jim this doesn't concern him and to walk away
jokes.punchline: Jim makes a nonsensical political reference that accidentally makes a good point about healthcare
jokes.explanation: Decent political absurdity but feels slightly forced. The healthcare punchline is clever but the communist Sweden setup is random.

jokes.text: Dwight: 'If we were, I wouldn't have to worry, 'cause we'd have universal health care'
jokes.setup: Dwight's communist Sweden comment
jokes.punchline: He actually understands healthcare benefits of the system he's criticizing
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - Dwight accidentally argues for the benefits of the system he was criticizing. Shows his confused politics.

jokes.text: From who, Dr. J?
jokes.setup: Darryl claims he has a doctor's note
jokes.punchline: Jim suggests it's from Dr. J (Julius Erving), implying it's fake
jokes.explanation: Solid quick joke with good economy. The Dr. J reference is dated but works for the character's skepticism.

jokes.text: Since when have you known Darryl to rush to do anything... other than to come up here for birthday cake? 'Y'all having birthday cake?'
jokes.setup: Jim questioning Darryl's story about rushing to fill an order
jokes.punchline: He does an impression of Darryl only being motivated by cake
jokes.explanation: Good character observation with a quotable impression. The setup effectively establishes Darryl's usual behavior patterns.

jokes.text: That's not a very good Darryl.
jokes.setup: Jim doing an impression of Darryl
jokes.punchline: Darryl critiques the impression of himself
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective meta-joke. Darryl's deadpan critique of his own impression has good economy.

jokes.text: Pam doing a noir detective impression: 'I'll punch you into mush, see?'
jokes.setup: Pam mentions being a fan of hardboiled detective novels
jokes.punchline: Does over-the-top 1940s tough guy impression
jokes.explanation: Unexpected character moment for Pam - showing a different side through her detective novel interest. The impression is charmingly awkward.

jokes.text: Yes, I do. No, I don't. Yes, I do. No, I don't. Catch you on the flippity-flop.
jokes.setup: David asks Michael to leave the meeting
jokes.punchline: Michael can't decide if he minds, then uses nonsensical slang
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael flip-flopping followed by completely made-up slang. 'Flippity-flop' is perfectly quotable nonsense.

jokes.text: Michael pretending to be on important phone call: 'You tell the mayor he just lost six votes'
jokes.setup: Michael trying to seem important outside the conference room
jokes.punchline: Fake phone call with absurdly low-stakes political threat
jokes.explanation: Michael's pathetic attempt to seem powerful. Six votes is hilariously small political influence.

jokes.text: Had to come in on a Saturday... to retrieve... I left my cell phone here.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining how chaotic work has been, building up to coming in on Saturday
jokes.punchline: He only came in to get his cell phone, not to work
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that undercuts Michael's attempt to seem hardworking. The pause before 'retrieve' sells the deflation perfectly.

jokes.text: So jealous of your boobs.
jokes.setup: Kevin presumably looking at Pam
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate and direct comment about her pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Standard Kevin inappropriate comment. While in character, it's not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Which is why we're having it in Niagara Falls.
jokes.setup: Pam explaining they realized most people couldn't make the drive to Niagara Falls
jokes.punchline: She states this as the reason FOR having it there, not against
jokes.explanation: Nice understated irony. Pam's deadpan delivery of the backwards logic works well for her character's occasional passive-aggressiveness.

jokes.text: But I'm not gonna go if Ryan doesn't go, because it's kind of a waste of time... That came out wrong.
jokes.setup: Kelly explaining her wedding attendance depends on Ryan
jokes.punchline: She accidentally calls Jim and Pam's wedding a waste of time, then tries to backtrack
jokes.explanation: Classic Kelly self-absorption. Her accidental insult followed by weak backtracking is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'm in this weird, flirty, nebulous... thing... with this cousin of mine. And it's a total mind f'er. Again with the cousin.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why his sales are down
jokes.punchline: He's having romantic issues with his cousin, and Phyllis's exasperated reaction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy that builds on established character weirdness. Phyllis's 'again with the cousin' sells the running gag aspect.

jokes.text: You know when they say it's so crazy it just might work? I don't believe that. I say go for the airtight plan.
jokes.setup: Michael rejecting the 'so crazy it might work' philosophy in favor of airtight plans
jokes.punchline: He immediately describes hiding in a cheese cart - the opposite of airtight
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic setup where Michael's stated philosophy immediately contradicts his actions. The juxtaposition is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Andy wheel me into the conference room inside a makeshift cheese cart... It is... just... elegant.
jokes.setup: Michael's plan to spy on the meeting
jokes.punchline: He describes this ridiculous scheme as 'elegant'
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael delusion - calling an obviously ridiculous plan 'elegant' with complete sincerity. The pause before 'elegant' is perfect.

jokes.text: Michael calling his cheese cart plan 'elegant'
jokes.setup: Michael's makeshift cheese cart spy plan
jokes.punchline: Describes this absurd plan as 'elegant'
jokes.explanation: Michael's misuse of 'elegant' for his ridiculous spying method. His confidence makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Andy's terrible French accent and elaborate cheese descriptions
jokes.setup: Andy executing Michael's cheese cart plan
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top performance with fake accent and made-up cheese knowledge
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy bit. Andy's commitment sells it but the concept is fairly predictable and goes on too long.

jokes.text: Also from the great city of Wisconsin
jokes.setup: Andy continuing his fake cheese expertise
jokes.punchline: He calls Wisconsin a city instead of a state
jokes.explanation: Simple geography mistake for a quick laugh. Not particularly clever but fits Andy's character trying too hard.

jokes.text: it's a lot of fun... to let the goldfish... take a little swim in the blue cheese
jokes.setup: Andy describing how to eat the cheese platter
jokes.punchline: He suggests dipping goldfish crackers in blue cheese dressing as if it's haute cuisine
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment where Andy treats junk food like fine dining. The goldfish imagery is mildly clever.

jokes.text: It sounds like a... EMD P40. That's a GE. The P40 is much higher pitched.
jokes.setup: Michael and David discussing train sounds
jokes.punchline: Their oddly specific technical knowledge about train models from just hearing them
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail showing their shared nerdiness. The specificity of the train models is funny in its unexpected precision.

jokes.text: Dwight and Jim's train expertise conversation
jokes.setup: Dwight and Jim discussing train sounds
jokes.punchline: They actually connect over shared train knowledge
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of genuine connection between Jim and Dwight over trains. Sweet but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: I'm rebuilding a turn-of-the-century steam engine in my slaughterhouse.
jokes.setup: Michael connecting with David over trains
jokes.punchline: He casually mentions having a slaughterhouse as his workshop
jokes.explanation: Perfectly casual delivery of completely absurd information. The juxtaposition of hobby trains and slaughterhouse is brilliantly unexpected.

jokes.text: Dwight: 'It's just a slaughterhouse, but, sure, any time'
jokes.setup: Jim expressing interest in seeing the steam engine
jokes.punchline: Dwight downplays having a slaughterhouse
jokes.explanation: Dwight's casual attitude toward owning a slaughterhouse. The understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Worker's comp my ass!
jokes.setup: Jim and Dwight catching Darryl not using crutches
jokes.punchline: Jim's uncharacteristic outburst of anger
jokes.explanation: Jim's rare moment of genuine anger is mildly funny due to character contrast, but the line itself isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: You gonna eat all that dog food yourself?
jokes.setup: Darryl being confronted about his fake injury
jokes.punchline: He insults them by calling their food dog food
jokes.explanation: Generic insult that serves the plot but isn't particularly witty. Functional but not memorable.

jokes.text: Dwight: 'Worker's comp my ass!'
jokes.setup: Catching Darryl faking his injury
jokes.punchline: Dwight's indignant reaction
jokes.explanation: Dwight's outrage is in character but not particularly creative. Standard reaction.

jokes.text: I once had a glass of cognac that cost $77, so...
jokes.setup: Pam mentioning the wedding costs $75 per person
jokes.punchline: Ryan's completely irrelevant and pretentious response about expensive alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan pretentiousness - making everything about his supposed sophistication. The non-sequitur quality makes it authentically obnoxious.

jokes.text: Jim is like... Big Bird. He is tall... and yellow... and very nice.
jokes.setup: Michael being asked to evaluate Jim's management potential
jokes.punchline: He compares Jim to Big Bird with increasingly absurd reasoning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael analogy that starts logical then becomes absurd. Jim being 'yellow' is completely random but delivered with such conviction it's hilarious.

jokes.text: Big Bird doesn't make the tough decisions. If I was going to put someone in charge, I would put Bert in charge.
jokes.setup: Michael continuing his Sesame Street analogy for office management
jokes.punchline: He seriously analyzes Sesame Street characters' leadership qualities
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the Big Bird analogy. Michael's serious consideration of Bert's management skills is peak absurdist character comedy.

jokes.text: Or I would put one of the real grown-ups, like Maria. Or Gordon, maybe.
jokes.setup: Michael still analyzing Sesame Street management options
jokes.punchline: He considers the human characters as 'real grown-ups' for management
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the Sesame Street bit. The logic of preferring human characters shows Michael's mind actually working through the analogy.

jokes.text: I'd have to talk to my mother... and my guy at H&R block.
jokes.setup: Michael being offered a promotion
jokes.punchline: He needs to consult his mother and tax preparer about a job decision
jokes.explanation: Good Michael childishness - treating a career decision like he needs parental permission. The H&R Block reference adds financial anxiety humor.

jokes.text: Example: he handed out jell-o shots... at the 23rd mile of the Steamtown Marathon.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why he didn't tell Michael about the job offer
jokes.punchline: His specific example of Michael's misguided helpfulness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael story - well-intentioned but completely inappropriate. The specificity of 'jell-o shots' and '23rd mile' makes it feel authentic and absurd.

jokes.text: Did you know that Lincoln's secretary was named Kennedy?
jokes.setup: Jim pressing Michael about what he said to Wallace
jokes.punchline: Michael deflects with a random historical factoid
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael deflection technique. The random trivia is appropriately off-topic but not particularly memorable on its own.

jokes.text: Why would you think a lady is me? Are you serious? Because you look exactly alike.
jokes.setup: Darryl angry about them harassing his sister
jokes.punchline: Jim and Dwight's oblivious response about the family resemblance
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of their detective work gone wrong. The logic is flawed but believably so for the characters' desperation.

jokes.text: Dwight and Jim mistaking Darryl's sister for him: 'Because you look exactly alike'
jokes.setup: They harassed Darryl's sister thinking she was him
jokes.punchline: Dwight doubles down claiming they look identical
jokes.explanation: Dwight's inability to admit the obvious mistake. The cringe comes from his doubling down on the error.

jokes.text: Absolutely... not! Like I said before, Jim is fantastic, and he deserves this job.
jokes.setup: Wallace asking if Michael is changing his mind about Jim
jokes.punchline: Michael's emphatic 'absolutely not' followed immediately by praising Jim
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect timing. Michael's enthusiastic contradiction of himself captures his panicked flip-flopping perfectly.

jokes.text: That file had been... falsified. Toby Flenderson is doing drugs.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to explain away Jim's bad performance review
jokes.punchline: He accuses Toby of drug use to discredit the report
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael escalation - when in trouble, blame Toby with increasingly absurd accusations. The pause before 'falsified' shows his desperation.

jokes.text: Darryl threatening to claim sexual harassment: 'I'm telling them you sexually harassed my sister'
jokes.setup: Dwight threatening to file complaint about Darryl's lying
jokes.punchline: Darryl counters with sexual harassment threat
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the complaint war. Darryl using the sister incident as leverage.

jokes.text: I'm telling them you sexually harassed my sister.
jokes.setup: Dwight threatening to file a complaint about Darryl's insurance fraud
jokes.punchline: Darryl threatens to accuse them of sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that fits the mutual assured destruction theme. Not particularly clever but serves the plot effectively.

jokes.text: No judges gonna believe that.
jokes.setup: Darryl threatening sexual harassment accusations
jokes.punchline: Jim's confident dismissal of the threat
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that serves the scene but isn't particularly witty. Jim's confidence is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: It would make me feel really good if you would punch me in the face.
jokes.setup: Michael feeling guilty about sabotaging Jim
jokes.punchline: He asks Jim to literally punch him as penance
jokes.explanation: Good Michael guilt response - his solution is characteristically physical and inappropriate. The directness of the request is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: That's what she said.
jokes.setup: Jim saying 'Instead, you screwed me?'
jokes.punchline: Michael's automatic 'That's what she said' in a completely inappropriate moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael reflexive inappropriate response. While predictable, the timing during a serious confrontation makes it perfectly wrong.

jokes.text: Co-manager and co-manager.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to establish hierarchy as 'manager and co-manager'
jokes.punchline: Wallace's flat correction that they're both co-managers
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Michael's ego. Wallace's matter-of-fact delivery cuts through Michael's delusion efficiently.

jokes.text: Co-manager of what? Of your butt! And your butt, and your butt...
jokes.setup: Someone questioning what Jim is co-manager of
jokes.punchline: Michael's childish 'your butt' response to everyone
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael immaturity. While not clever, his regression to playground insults during a professional announcement is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'll just have whatever's fanciest, unless there's ribs, I'll get the other information the day of. I'll text you.
jokes.setup: Pam trying to get proper RSVP information for her wedding
jokes.punchline: Creed's completely unhelpful and selfish response to wedding planning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed selfishness and lack of social awareness. His complete disregard for wedding planning etiquette is authentically inconsiderate.

jokes.text: You're going to text me... the morning of my wedding to ask for directions?
jokes.setup: Creed's casual approach to wedding logistics
jokes.punchline: Pam's incredulous breakdown of how inappropriate this is
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to Creed's absurdity. Pam's mounting frustration with wedding planning is relatable and well-executed.
